items.title: How to Turn a Boring Corporate Strategy Into a Shared Team Story
items.summary: This article highlights the importance of framing corporate strategy as a shared team story rather than a top-down mandate. By using language that emphasizes milestones and goals the team actively wants to achieve, you can foster greater buy-in and engagement.  Shifting from abstract corporate directives to concrete, team-oriented objectives is key to driving motivation and productivity.

items.title: AI Accelerates Building. It Does Not Accelerate Commitment.
items.summary: The article emphasizes that AI tools can speed up development but not decision-making or commitment.

items.title: AI Agents Don’t Need Your Permission Anymore — What That Means If You’re a Product Manager
items.summary: The article highlights that AI agents now operate without needing user intervention, significantly altering the Product Manager's role.

items.title: I’m Standing Inside Bell Labs’ Legacy. And Thinking About AI.
items.summary: The article describes a visit to Bell Works, a former Bell Labs facility now housing various tech companies. The author reflects on the significance of AI in light of Bell Labs' historical innovation, suggesting a potential resurgence of groundbreaking research and development. This highlights the importance of anticipating future technological shifts and fostering environments conducive to innovation, particularly in areas like AI.

items.title: 📈 Data to start your week – The jet fuel inflection point
items.summary: This article highlights a potential inflection point in energy markets driven by geopolitical conflict, suggesting a shift in energy supply and demand dynamics. For Product Managers, this signals a need to monitor external factors impacting user behavior and potentially adjust product roadmaps to address changing needs related to energy costs or availability. Understanding these macro trends is crucial for anticipating future user needs and maintaining product relevance.

items.title: Google's Nest Doorbell
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: 🎙️ This week on How I AI: Claude Cowork tutorial for non-engineers + Build your own Slack inbox (for $0)
items.summary: This article promotes a tutorial from How I AI focusing on using Claude, an AI assistant, for collaborative workflows, specifically designed for non-technical users. It also highlights a method for creating a custom Slack inbox for free, offering practical productivity tips.  Product Managers can leverage these resources to explore AI tools for team collaboration and streamline communication processes.

items.title: Claude Cowork 101: How to automate your workday without touching code | JJ Englert (Tenex)
items.summary: This article demonstrates how to leverage Claude Cowork to automate repetitive tasks across various business tools like email and Slack, significantly boosting productivity. It highlights a no-code approach, allowing product managers and teams to integrate AI capabilities into their workflows without requiring technical expertise. The core insight is that AI automation can be readily implemented to streamline daily operations and free up valuable time for strategic work.

items.title: Mythos, Muse, and the Opportunity Cost of Compute
items.summary: The article argues that Aggregation Theory still holds when compute resources are scarce, because firms that can manage and prioritize demand will control the limited supply and gain market power. For product managers, this highlights the strategic advantage of shaping user demand to secure access to constrained compute resources.

items.title: iRobot: Colin Angle. How The Roomba Became a Household Icon
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: How We Built a Voice Agent on Chatzy.ai in Just One Week
items.summary: A hands-on account of shipping a voice agent in one week using AI-assisted development, covering the key architectural decisions and tradeoffs made under time pressure. The team prioritised speed over perfection, using existing APIs and low-code tools to validate the concept before investing in custom infrastructure. A useful case study for PMs evaluating rapid prototyping approaches for voice and conversational AI features.

items.title: Everything is Connected: A Preview of My New Book
items.summary: A preview of an upcoming book arguing that product, design, engineering, and business outcomes are deeply interconnected systems rather than separate concerns. The author challenges the tendency to optimise individual parts in isolation, which often creates local wins but systemic failures. PMs will find value in the systems-thinking framework proposed for navigating cross-functional complexity.

items.title: Most Businesses Don't Need More Posts. They Need This Instead.
items.summary: Most businesses default to producing more content when their marketing underperforms, but the real problem is usually a lack of strategic positioning and clear value proposition. The author argues that clarity of message and audience targeting outperforms volume every time. PMs building growth loops should consider this when evaluating content as an acquisition channel.

items.title: I Built a Fitness Tracker for How You Speak
items.summary: The founder of ExecReps.ai shares how they overcame the 'Gulf of Evaluation' problem — showing users too much data with too little meaning. After building an impressive analytics dashboard, they realised users couldn't answer a simple question: am I improving? The key PM lesson is that users want answers, not data, and simplicity in measurement design drives better outcomes than comprehensiveness.

items.title: Streaks, Nudges, and the Behavioral Science of Showing Up
items.summary: A founder's account of the internal debate around adding streaks to ExecReps.ai, exploring the tension between building a healthy habit product and creating guilt-driven engagement. Drawing on Duolingo's experience and behavioural science research, the team evaluated whether streaks help users build genuine practice or just anxiety. An essential read for PMs working on retention and engagement mechanics.

items.title: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
items.summary: Keith Rabois shares his contrarian views on building products in the AI era, including his 'barrels vs. ammunition' hiring framework and why he believes talking to customers actively harms consumer product development. He argues AI is fundamentally collapsing the traditional PM role, and that the best builders today work from strong conviction rather than user feedback. A provocative perspective that challenges conventional product wisdom.

items.title: 🔮 Exponential View #569: When the future is uncertain, what do you teach?
items.summary: The article discusses adapting to rapidly changing environments and the importance of leveraging AI tools to stay ahead.

items.title: Community Wisdom: Vibe coding as a team sport, teaching interviewers to surface real signal, and more
items.summary: A curated collection of insights from Lenny's Slack community covering practical topics including using AI-assisted coding collaboratively, improving interview signal quality, and making leadership offsites more effective. Community members share first-hand experiences and frameworks that go beyond theory. Useful for PMs looking for peer-tested approaches to common team challenges.

items.title: 2026.15: Myth and Mythos
items.summary: Ben Thompson explores how dominant tech narratives shape product strategy and competitive dynamics in the AI era, drawing parallels between mythological storytelling and modern platform wars. He argues that companies which control the prevailing narrative gain strategic leverage well beyond their actual technical capabilities. Essential context for PMs navigating AI-driven market shifts and positioning decisions.

items.title: The fall of the house of product:  A heartfelt goodbye
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: The financial blind spot that gets product managers fired
items.summary: Many product managers excel at product metrics like NPS and activation rates but are dangerously unaware of the financial metrics boards actually care about — particularly LTV-to-CAC ratio and payback period. The author argues this blind spot is one of the leading reasons experienced PMs get cut in budget reviews. PMs who want to be taken seriously at the executive level need to own their product's financial narrative.

items.title: Anamap
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: Okta: Principal Product Manager, LogIn
items.summary: This job posting highlights the critical role of a Principal Product Manager focused on login experiences, specifically within Okta's Auth0 platform. The position emphasizes ownership of a high-impact area – the 'front door' for millions of users – and requires defining secure, customizable, and performant authentication flows that balance security with a seamless user experience for major brands. It's a technically focused role demanding expertise in building secure-by-design authentication solutions.

items.title: Signifyd: Senior Product Manager, Identity & Intelligence Platform
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: Commercial vs Internal Products
items.summary: Marty Cagan examines the fundamental differences between building commercial products for paying customers and internal tools for employees, arguing that the constraints, incentives, and success metrics are so different they require distinct PM skill sets. Internal products often lack the forcing function of market competition, which leads to lower quality and slower iteration. A useful framework for PMs transitioning between these contexts.

items.title: An Interview with New York Times CEO Meredith Kopit Levien About Betting on Humans With Expertise
items.summary: The NYT CEO discusses the company's strategy of doubling down on human expertise and trusted journalism as a competitive moat against AI-generated content. She argues that in a world of abundant AI content, scarcity of credible human expertise becomes the premium product. PMs in media or content-adjacent products will find this a valuable case study in differentiation strategy.

items.title: Advice Line with Steve Ells of Chipotle
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: How quantum computing is transforming  data centers and energy
items.summary: Summary not available.

items.title: Product Roadmaps: How the Best Product Teams Plan for Uncertainty
items.summary: This article highlights the pitfalls of poorly constructed product roadmaps, exemplified by a scenario where prioritization was arbitrary and lacked a clear rationale. It underscores the need for a more robust and strategic approach to roadmap planning, especially when navigating uncertainty. For Product Managers, this emphasizes the importance of data-driven prioritization and stakeholder alignment, moving beyond simple feature lists.

items.title: I built a custom Slack inbox. It was easier than you'd think. | Yash Tekriwal (Clay)
items.summary: Yash Tekriwal from Clay shares how he used AI agents to reduce 150+ daily Slack notifications to ~30 actionable items by building a custom digest and Kanban dashboard. The system categorises messages into action-required, need-to-read, and FYI buckets automatically. A practical example of how PMs can use AI tools to reclaim focus and reduce notification fatigue without waiting for their company to solve it.

items.title: Anthropic's New Model, The Mythos Wolf, Glasswing and Alignment
items.summary: Ben Thompson analyses Anthropic's latest model release in the context of the broader AI alignment debate and competitive dynamics among frontier AI labs. He examines how safety-focused positioning affects product strategy and market perception for companies like Anthropic. Essential reading for PMs tracking the AI landscape and thinking about how foundation model choices affect their product roadmap.

items.title: FOMO - All Things Product Podcast with Teresa Torres & Petra Wille
items.summary: This podcast episode addresses the overwhelming nature of the rapidly evolving AI landscape for product professionals. It acknowledges the excitement of new AI tools while highlighting the challenge of staying current without experiencing burnout. The conversation likely offers strategies for navigating this constant influx of information and prioritizing impactful AI adoption.

items.title: How will OpenAI compete?
items.summary: Benedict Evans argues that OpenAI faces four fundamental strategic challenges: no clear competitive moat, a rapidly shifting product landscape, the need to cross the chasm without existing distribution, and the difficulty of building consumer products on top of commodity models. He questions whether OpenAI's massive user base translates into durable strategic advantage. A clear-eyed analysis of the competitive dynamics shaping the AI product landscape.

items.title: Product Coaching and AI
items.summary: Marty Cagan explores how AI is changing the nature of product coaching and mentorship, arguing that while AI can accelerate skill development in some areas, the fundamentally human aspects of product leadership — judgement, empathy, and stakeholder navigation — remain beyond AI's reach. He suggests coaches should focus on these irreplaceable skills rather than competing with AI on technical knowledge. A thoughtful perspective for PMs investing in their own development.
